channel.title: SoloTrack Blog
channel.description: Practical writing for student pilots: flight logging, currency tracking, FAR 61.109 progress, and how SoloTrack fits into training.

item.title: Checkride preparation: what your DPE expects and how to organize your logbook
item.description: How to prepare for your private pilot checkride — the experience requirements, endorsements, and a clean logbook your DPE can actually review.

item.title: How to import your logbook into SoloTrack (CSV from paper, ForeFlight, LogTen, MyFlightBook)
item.description: Step-by-step: export your existing flights to CSV and import them into SoloTrack with duplicate detection — from paper, ForeFlight, LogTen, or MyFlightBook.

item.title: Night flight requirements for the private pilot certificate (FAR 61.109(a)(2)(ii))
item.description: The night dual, night cross-country, and towered-airport landing requirements every PPL student needs — broken down in plain English.

item.title: FAR 61.57 currency explained: day, night, and instrument
item.description: A plain-English guide to FAR 61.57 day, night, and instrument currency — what counts, the 90-day and six-month windows, and how to stay legal to carry passengers.

item.title: What SoloTrack does (and what it does not)
item.description: A clear scope of what SoloTrack is — a focused iOS flight logbook for student pilots — and the things it intentionally is not.

item.title: FAR 61.109 explained: the six requirements every PPL student needs to track
item.description: A plain-English walk-through of the FAA's aeronautical-experience requirements for the private pilot certificate, mapped to how SoloTrack tracks them.

item.title: A privacy-first flight logbook: how SoloTrack handles your data
item.description: Why SoloTrack stores your flight log on-device, how optional iCloud sync works, and what we deliberately do not do with your data.
